format:
  html: 
    toc: true
    other-links:
      - text: "PDF with R"
        href: "R/multi-language-test-5-R.pdf"
        icon: file-pdf
      - text: "PDF with Stata"

Here, I run Stata parallel to R and Python using `Statamarkdown`. 


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(Statamarkdown)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collectcode = TRUE)
```
:::


:::{.panel-tabset group="languages"}

## R


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
print("Hello, R world!")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "Hello, R world!"
```


:::
:::


## Stata


::: {.cell}

```{.stata .cell-code}
dis "Hello, Stata world!"
```


::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Hello, Stata world!
```


:::
:::


## Python


::: {.cell}

```{.python .cell-code}
print("Hello, Python world!")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Hello, Python world!
```


:::
:::

:::
